title: Incident
description: Correlated group of events + findings + actions that share a root operational condition. Owns a state machine (open -> triaging -> mitigated -> resolved -> closed) so postmortems, on-call handoffs, and after-action reviews have a durable, incident-indexed anchor. Distinct from a single Event: an Incident groups many events but exists exactly once per correlation-key set.

incident_id.description: Deterministic UUID5 derived from `correlation_keys`. Re-emitting the same key set yields the same incident_id (idempotent grouping).

state.description: Current lifecycle state. Transitions are enforced by the core/incident state machine; illegal transitions raise IncidentTransitionError.

severity.description: Sev1 = customer-visible outage; sev5 = informational. Set at open; may be adjusted via re-open. Mirrors PagerDuty / Datadog vocabulary.

opened_at.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the open transition.

mitigated_at.description: UTC timestamp when the incident entered `mitigated` (user impact stopped even if root cause not yet resolved).

resolved_at.description: UTC timestamp when the incident entered `resolved` (root cause remediated).

closed_at.description: UTC timestamp when the incident entered `closed` (postmortem published, after-action items landed).

correlation_keys.description: The keys that group events into this incident (e.g. `resource_id:foo`, `deployment_id:bar`). Determines `incident_id` via UUID5(NAMESPACE_URL, sorted(keys)).

member_event_ids.description: Every event that this incident groups. Appended atomically as new correlated events arrive.

related_finding_ids.description: Finding ids produced against events in this incident.

related_action_ids.description: Action ids executed (or attempted, incl. shadow) against this incident.

assignee_oid.description: Entra OID of the current incident commander. Distinct from any submitter to preserve the no-self-approval invariant during any HIL step.

mitigation_summary.description: Free-text mitigation note captured at the mitigated transition.

postmortem_ref.description: Git-managed path to the published postmortem (e.g. `rule-catalog/postmortems/<incident-id>.md`). Populated at close.
